source_title: FiberTak SpeedBridge Rotator Cuff Repair (technique guide)
text: FiberTak Âź SpeedBridgeâą Rotator Cuff Repair Surgical Technique 02 | FiberTakÂź SpeedBridgeâą Rotator Cuff Repair FiberTakÂź SpeedBridgeâą Repair Advantages Íž Biomechanical repair strength is comparable to traditional SpeedBridge repair 1 Íž Multiple fixation points and additional FiberTape sutures increase tendon-to-bone compression 2 Íž Contact area of the tendon-to-footprint interface increased 14% compared to traditional hard- bodied anchors 2 Íž Secure lateral fixation with trusted SwiveLock anchors Enhancements Knotless 2.6 FiberTak RC anchors are adaptable and can conform to traditional repair techniques while providing the additional benefits of tensionable knotless technology for incorporating biologics or increasing medial-row compression. As the next evolution of knotless rotator cuff repair, the FiberTak SpeedBridge technique is completed with 2.6 FiberTak RC soft anchors on the medial row and FiberTapeÂź sutures fixated laterally with trusted SwiveLockÂź anchors. This knotless repair creates a quick, secure construct in as few as three suture-passing steps. The small size of the 2.6 FiberTak RC anchors allows for three points of medial-row fixation where previously only two fixation points could be achieved. Double-Pulley Rip-Stop With ArthroFLEX Âź Dermal Allograft Augmentation Independent Mattress Rip-Stops Canopy Augmentation With ArthroFLEX Dermal Allograft Independent Mattress Stitches ArthroFLEX is a registered trademark of LifeNet Health. FiberTakÂź SpeedBridgeâą Rotator Cuff Repair | 03 2.6 FiberTakÂź RC Soft Anchors These anchors are available with fixed 1.7 mm FiberTape Âź sutureâwith joined tails for simplified suture-passingâand the option of a sliding SutureTape or tensionable knotless mechanism. Sliding 1.3 SutureTape Incorporate sliding SutureTape for knotted medial-row compression variations or remove if it is not needed for the repair. Self-Punching Inserters All 2.6 FiberTak anchors are loaded on self-punching inserters, which eliminate the need to predrill or prepunch a socket for anchor insertion, reducing surgical steps. Use these inserters with or without a guide; drills and punches are available for hard bone. Tensionable Knotless Mechanism With the tensionable knotless mechanism, complete a variety of medial-row compression variations and easily incorporate biologics. SwiveLock Lateral-Row Anchors Íž Trusted fixation with more than 10 million anchors used since 20063 Íž Available with #2 suture for tensionable knotless dog- ear or cable fixation Joined FiberTape Suture Tails Íž Simplified suture-passing Íž Shuttle both limbs of FiberTape suture with one pass 2.6 FiberTak RC Soft Anchors Knotless 2.6 FiberTak RC Soft Anchors 04 | FiberTakÂź SpeedBridgeâą Rotator Cuff Repair FiberTakÂź SpeedBridgeâą Technique: Medial Row FiberTakÂź SpeedBridgeâą Rotator Cuff Repair | 05 01 While viewing through a posterolateral portal, prepare the rotator cuff footprint and identify the posteromedial anchor insertion point. Using a mallet, insert and advance the 2.6 FiberTak RC anchor into bone; stop at the insertion zone laser line. If hard bone is encountered, use a punch or drill to create a socket to ease anchor insertion. 03 Load the single end of FiberTapeÂź suture onto a Scorpionâą suture passer and advance through the rotator cuff. Pull the suture to smoothly lead both FiberTape suture limbs through the tissue. Note: Drilling in very hard bone may require cycling the drill while maintaining consistent alignment of the drill guide. Increased size, hard bone drills are also available for use. 02 Once the anchor is fully inserted, remove the suture retention ring and inserter. Grasp all limbs of the suture and pull slowly to set the anchor. Repeat steps 1 and 2 for middle and anterior medial-row anchors. Once the anchors are inserted, remove the sliding SutureTape. 04 Cut off the joined suture tail, allowing each suture limb to be separated for lateral fixation. Tension each suture limb independently to remove any potential slack beneath the tendon. FiberTakÂź SpeedBridgeâą Technique: Medial Row 06 | FiberTakÂź SpeedBridgeâą Rotator Cuff Repair FiberTakÂź SpeedBridgeâą Technique: Lateral Row FiberTakÂź SpeedBridgeâą Rotator Cuff Repair | 07 01 Retrieve one FiberTapeÂź suture tail from each medial anchor and preload them through the SwiveLock Âź anchor eyelet. Using a punch, prepare a bone socket about 5-10 mm lateral to the edge of the tuberosity. Note: Use a hemostat to secure sutures in place while preparing a bone socket. 03 Completely advance the driver into the bone socket, beyond the first laser line and until the anchor body contacts bone. Evaluate suture tension. If tension is not adequate, back the driver out and readjust the tension. Note: Do not attempt to apply tension to the sutures with the eyelet in the bone socket. 02 Bring the eyelet of the implant to the edge of the bone socket and remove slack from each of the FiberTape suture limbs individually. Apply tension to the FiberTape sutures so that the tissue is reduced and compressed to the bone. 04 Make sure the tip of the anchor body is in contact with bone. Hold the thumb pad steady and rotate the driver handle in a clockwise direction to insert the anchor body until it is flush with the bone.
